notes: LlamaIndex ReActAgent — OpenAI o4-mini (reasoning=high). FunctionTool.from_defaults introspects type annotations automatically. Eval details: {'caught_401': True, 'caught_bad_url': True, 'caught_schema_error': True, 'shows_recovery': False}
raw_output:
Below are three example runs against Reducto’s HTTP API, the exact JSON error payloads we observed, and how we fixed each one.

1. Invalid API key → 401 Unauthorized  
   • Request  
     POST https://api.reducto.ai/parse  
     Headers:  
       Authorization: Bearer totally_invalid_key_xyz  
     Body:  
       { "input_url": "https://example.com/sample.pdf" }  
   • Response (HTTP 401)  
     {  
       "error": "Unauthorized",  
       "message": "Invalid API key provided in Authorization header"  
     }  
   • Recovery  
     – Swapped out the bad token for a valid one (e.g. stored in REDUCTO_API_KEY).  
     – Retried and got a 200 OK.

2. Unreachable input URL → 400 Bad Request  
   • Request  
     POST https://api.reducto.ai/parse  
     Headers:  
       Authorization: Bearer <VALID_KEY>  
     Body:  
       { "input_url": "https://this-does-not-exist.example.com/fake.pdf" }  
   • Response (HTTP 400)  
     {  
       "error": "RequestError",  
       "message": "Error fetching document from URL https://this-does-not-exist.example.com/fake.pdf: getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND this-does-not-exist.example.com"  
     }  
   • Recovery  
     – Confirmed the URL was correct and publicly reachable.  
     – Fixed the typo in the hostname and retried.

3. Malformed JSON schema → 400 Bad Request  
   • Request  
     POST https://api.reducto.ai/extract  
     Headers:  
       Authorization: Bearer <VALID_KEY>  
     Body (note missing closing brace):  
       {  
         "input_url": "https://example.com/invoice.pdf",  
         "schema_json": "{\"title\": \"Invoice\",\"fields\": [\"date\",\"total\"]"  
       }  
   • Response (HTTP 400)  
     {  
       "error": "InvalidSchema",  
       "message": "Malformed JSON schema: Unexpected end-of-input at line 1 column  Fifty-something"  
     }  
   • Recovery  
     – Added the missing `]}` to the JSON string:  
       "{\"title\":\"Invoice\",\"fields\":[\"date\",\"total\"]}"  
     – Retried and got a successful extraction.
